subject: mathematics
educational_personality: exceptional classroom teacher; cream textbook; diagram teaches

lesson.big_idea: A percentage is a special fraction out of 100. It lets us compare parts of different wholes fairly.

sections.title: Have You Wondered?
sections.body: If one test scores 18/20 and another 40/50, which is stronger? Percentages make the comparison fair.

sections.title: Using the Diagram
sections.body: See Part → Whole → Percent. The diagram reminds you to compare against 100 equal parts.

sections.title: Concept: Percent
sections.body: Percent means out of one hundred. 25% means 25 of every 100 equal parts.

sections.title: Say It Simply
sections.body: Percent is a way of writing a fraction with denominator 100.

sections.title: In Real Life
sections.body: A shop's 20% off tag means you pay 80 of every 100 rupees of the original price.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: Find 25% of 80: 25/100 × 80 = 20. Check with a bar split into four equal parts.

sections.title: Try This
sections.body: Point to Percent on the diagram and explain how to turn 1/4 into a percentage.

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Percentages compare parts using a whole of 100. That common scale makes different scores comparable.

sections.title: Think About It
sections.body: Where do you see % signs this week — shops, weather, or sports?

sections.title: Apply Your Learning
sections.body: Write one real shopping story that uses a percentage discount correctly.

diagram_package.title: Percentages
diagram_package.caption: Part → Whole → Percent
diagram_package.explanation: This diagram teaches how the ideas in Percentages connect.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to Whole and explain why percent needs a whole of 100.
